source.notes: Production incident with gateway error logs showing 429 with 'Extra usage is required for long context requests' being surfaced to users as 'API rate limit reached. Please try again later.' — wrong advice that cannot be resolved by waiting or retrying.

hazard.summary: Structural rate limit (billing/context tier) misclassified as transient rate limit by message-pattern matching, causing wrong user guidance — 'try again later' instead of 'reduce context or enable billing'
signals: rawError=429 {"type":"error","error":{"type":"rate_limit_error","message":"Extra usage is required for long context requests."}}
User sees: API rate limit reached. Please try again later.
RATE_LIMIT_SPECIFIC_HINT_RE only matches time-based patterns (minutes, seconds, try again in, plan, quota)
Structural rate limit message contains no time signals — falls through to generic copy
Retrying will never help — context length does not change between retries

constraints: Structural rate limits (billing/tier/context) must be classified as STOP — not WAIT
Message-pattern classifiers must distinguish time-based signals from billing/tier signals before applying copy
User-facing guidance for structural limits must surface the correct action: reduce context or enable billing
Raw provider error messages must not be collapsed into generic 'try again later' when the error is non-retryable
Classification must run before copy generation — not after
knobs: RATE_LIMIT_SPECIFIC_HINT_RE — extend to include billing/tier signal patterns alongside time patterns
formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy — add dedicated branch for structural limits before time-pattern gate
structural_rate_limit_patterns — configurable list of message substrings that indicate non-retryable billing/tier limits
verification: 429 with 'Extra usage is required for long context requests' → classified as STOP, not WAIT
User-facing copy surfaces 'reduce context or enable Extra Usage billing' — not 'try again later'
429 with 'Rate limit reached... try again in 5.5s' → still classified as WAIT (regression check)
Raw provider error message preserved and surfaced when structural limit detected
No retry attempted after structural limit classification
notes: This receipt documents a new sub-class of 429 misclassification: message-content classification failure. Previous receipts document WAIT/STOP confusion based on Retry-After header presence and value. This case has no Retry-After header — the structural vs transient distinction must be made from the error message content itself. The 'Extra usage required' message is Anthropic-specific but the pattern generalizes: any provider that returns billing/tier errors as 429 without a short Retry-After is vulnerable to the same misclassification. Related active PR: openclaw/openclaw#54594.
